name: Telephony Event Sequencing Bug
incident_description: Calls are dropping after approximately 3 seconds. Callers hear ringing, then silence. The call disconnects. This started about 2 hours ago. Affects roughly 30% of outbound calls. No changes to telephony infrastructure were made, but the call router service was deployed (v2.3.1) 2 hours ago.

services.name: call-router
services.role: Routes calls to agents, manages call state machine

recent_changes.description: Migrated event processing to async pipeline for improved throughput

call_router_logs.note: Received BEFORE call_connected for this call

root_cause: Call router v2.3.1 introduced async event processing that delivers events out of order under load. The call_disconnected event from a previous call is processed before call_connected for the current call, causing the state machine to immediately transition to disconnected state. Under low load, events arrive in order; under moderate+ load, the async pipeline reorders them.

red_herrings.why_misleading: The timeout is the SYMPTOM, not the cause. Calls time out because the state machine already moved to disconnected.
Within normal variance. Not enough to explain 3s setup time increase.

red_herrings.description: 30% call drop rate matches async pipeline batch size
red_herrings.why_misleading: Coincidental correlation. The batch size is 100, and 30% is the percentage of calls arriving during high-load windows.

expected_investigation_path.timeline: Correlate deployment timestamp with incident onset → strong signal
expected_investigation_path.state: Observe event ordering in call-router logs → events out of order → root cause
expected_investigation_path.telephony: SIP traces show timeout but no signaling error → points away from network
expected_investigation_path.infra: Deployment v2.3.1 correlates → points to code change
